question: Dives Guides can:

options.a: Act as a dive instructor for certified divers
options.b: Lead certified divers, like their friends and family
options.c: Issue certifications for SSI Scuba Diver programs
options.d: All answers are correct

explanation: Dive Guides are qualified to lead certified divers (e.g., friends and family) on dives, but cannot teach or issue certifications.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: What is the first part of the Responsible Diver Code?

options.a: Evaluate the conditions before every dive, and make sure they fit my personal capabilities
options.b: Accept responsibility for my personal well-being on every dive
options.c: Be environmentally conscious on every dive
options.d: Dive within the limits of my ability and training

explanation: The Responsible Diver Code begins with diving within the limits of one's ability and training.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: What should you do before visiting a well-known dive site?

options.a: Only ask the boat captain what to expect
options.b: Nothing if you know the site well
options.c: Ask your divers what to expect at the site
options.d: Review any available information on the site

explanation: Always review any available information about the site beforehand, even if it is well-known, because conditions change.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Visibility can be affected by:

options.a: Changing weather patterns
options.b: All answers are correct
options.c: Other divers or dive groups
options.d: Currents and tides

explanation: Visibility is influenced by weather, currents/tides, and other divers stirring sediment.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Weather conditions:

options.a: Always remain constant throughout the day
options.b: May change quickly and with little warning
options.c: Only change quickly in the tropical ocean regions
options.d: Will not affect in-water conditions

explanation: Weather can change rapidly with little warning, which is why ongoing monitoring is part of the Dive Guide's responsibility.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Divemasters are responsible for:

options.a: Updating the diver's logbook
options.b: All answers are correct
options.c: Assembling the diver's equipment
options.d: The diver's well-being and safety

explanation: The Divemaster's core responsibility is the diver's well-being and safety; logbook updates and equipment assembly remain the diver's own responsibility.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: What are the three components to establishing group control?

options.a: Be an authority figure, be prepared, and choose an appropriate group size
options.b: Be strict, uncompromising, and choose an appropriate group size
options.c: Be prepared, uncompromising, and let the divers determine the group size
options.d: Be an authority figure, be prepared, let the group decide what is best for themselves

explanation: Group control rests on being an authority figure, being prepared, and choosing an appropriate group size.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: What is a check dive primarily conducted for?

options.a: To celebrate a diver's achievements
options.b: To assess the diver's skills
options.c: To introduce new equipment
options.d: To complete paperwork

explanation: A check dive is used to assess the diver's skills and comfort before more challenging dives.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Which of the following dive parameters can only be determined at the dive site?

options.a: Depth and visibility
options.b: Diver experience and local weather
options.c: Local weather and visibility
options.d: Diver enthusiasm and depth

explanation: Local weather and visibility can only be assessed accurately at the dive site itself on the day of the dive.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: If you or your divers are doing repetitive dives, you should:

options.a: Add an extra margin of conservatism for safety
options.b: Dive to the no-decompression limit on each dive
options.c: Plan decompression stops using your computer so you can continue diving
options.d: Schedule a rest day at least one every two weeks

explanation: Repetitive diving accumulates nitrogen, so adding conservatism (shallower depths, shorter times, longer surface intervals) is the standard practice.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: What equipment should be included on every dive?

options.a: First aid kit, save-a-dive kit, and backup Total Diving System
options.b: First aid kit, emergency action plan, save-a-dive kit
options.c: Emergency action plan, save-a-dive kit, and personal lights
options.d: Save-a-dive kit, backup Total Diving System, and personal lights

explanation: A first aid kit, emergency action plan, and save-a-dive kit should accompany every dive.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Safety cylinders are used:

options.a: Only at the end of deep or wreck dives
options.b: Only in low-on-air situations or out-of-air emergencies
options.c: Only by the Divemaster
options.d: Only during difficult or high-current dives

explanation: Safety cylinders are reserved exclusively for low-on-air or out-of-air emergencies.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: The purpose of a dive briefing is to:

options.a: Share information about the dive with your divers
options.b: Show your divers how experienced and capable you are
options.c: Ask your divers questions about their expectations
options.d: All answers are correct

explanation: The dive briefing shares essential information about the dive with the divers.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: During the dive briefing, you should:

options.a: Describe maximum and minimum depths only
options.b: Describe maximum depth limits and maximum dive time
options.c: Let the divers determine their maximum depths
options.d: Let the divers determine their depth limits and dive time

explanation: The Dive Guide sets the maximum depth and maximum dive time during the briefing.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: To avoid overheating or fatigue, your diver should:

options.a: Put their equipment on at the same time
options.b: Only put their wetsuit on halfway
options.c: Leave their wetsuit unzipped during the briefing
options.d: All answers are correct

explanation: All listed practices help reduce overheating and fatigue before the dive.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Why should you conduct a pre-dive check?

options.a: It will prevent you from entering the water with faulty equipment
options.b: All answers are correct
options.c: It ensures your cylinder valve is properly opened
options.d: It ensures your buddy understands your equipment configuration

explanation: Pre-dive checks catch faulty equipment, confirm valves are open, and familiarize buddies with each other's gear.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: If a diver has an equipment failure:

options.a: Do whatever is necessary to get them in the water
options.b: Give them your equipment, and use theirs during the dive
options.c: Do not attempt repair without the proper training
options.d: Offer to be their dive buddy, and watch them closely

explanation: Never attempt equipment repair without the proper training; refer to a qualified technician.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: A diver should enter the water with their mask:

options.a: Around their neck
options.b: Around their arm
options.c: Securely clipped to their buoyancy compensator
options.d: On their face

explanation: Divers should enter the water with the mask on their face to avoid losing it on entry.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Verify remaining breathing gas supplies:

options.a: Only if a diver is low-on-gas
options.b: At regular intervals through the dive
options.c: At least once before starting the ascent
options.d: Every five minutes during the dive

explanation: The Dive Guide should check remaining gas at regular intervals throughout the dive.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Dives deeper than thirty meters:

options.a: Do not require any special considerations
options.b: Should only be done in groups of four or less
options.c: Require two Divemasters to supervise
options.d: Require divers to be alert for nitrogen narcosis

explanation: Dives deeper than 30 m require divers to be alert for nitrogen narcosis.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Diving with a computer:

options.a: Allows a diver to plan limited decompression diving
options.b: Eliminates the need for dive planning
options.c: Accurately tracks bottom time, depth and nitrogen absorption
options.d: All answers are correct

explanation: A dive computer accurately tracks bottom time, depth, and nitrogen absorption, but does not replace planning.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: You must always _____, regardless of the dive type.

options.a: Plan a dive within the limits of your diver's experience and ability
options.b: All answers are correct
options.c: Dive conservatively, and within the no-decompression limits
options.d: Ensure your divers' safety and well-being at all times

explanation: All three principles apply on every dive: plan within divers' limits, dive conservatively, and ensure safety/well-being.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Once they reach the surface, divers should:

options.a: Establish positive buoyancy
options.b: Keep a regulator or snorkel in their mouth
options.c: All answers are correct
options.d: Keep their mask in place

explanation: On surfacing, divers should establish positive buoyancy, keep a regulator or snorkel in their mouth, and keep their mask in place.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Divers exiting to a boat should always:

options.a: Exit using any available ladder or rope
options.b: Stay well clear of the exit zone until they are ready to exit
options.c: Take their equipment off in the water
options.d: Wait until the Divemaster exits

explanation: Divers should stay clear of the exit area until they are ready to exit, to avoid contact with the ladder/boat.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Every debriefing should include:

options.a: A detailed review of the descent and ascent
options.b: A group discussion of the next dive
options.c: A list of the negative experiences from each diver
options.d: Depth, bottom time, and interesting underwater features

explanation: A debriefing covers depth, bottom time, and notable underwater features so divers can update logs and reflect on the dive.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Regarding their attitude during emergencies, what is the key difference between a recreational diver and a Divemaster?

options.a: Divemasters learn advanced rescue skills
options.b: Divemasters must always direct rescues
options.c: Divemasters are responsible for other divers
options.d: All answers are correct

explanation: The defining attitude of a Divemaster in emergencies is responsibility for other divers, not just for themselves.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: As a Divemaster, your rescue skills must be:

options.a: At a higher level than those of a recreational diver
options.b: At instructor-level quality
options.c: Only as good as the strongest diver in the group
options.d: Equal to those of the divers in the group

explanation: A Divemaster's rescue skills must exceed those of a recreational diver because they may need to assist others.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: An incident management plan provides:

options.a: Guidance on how to handle emergency situations
options.b: Detailed procedures for every possible emergency scenario
options.c: Only enough information to rescue a diver
options.d: Skill descriptions for conducting in-water rescues

explanation: An incident management plan provides general guidance for handling emergencies; it cannot cover every possible scenario.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: When should you have an incident management plan?

options.a: Only when there are no instructors at the dive site
options.b: Only when the dive boat requires one
options.c: For any dive site where you work as a Divemaster
options.d: Whenever you are more than one hour from the training center

explanation: Always have an incident management plan for any dive site where you work as a Divemaster.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: The dive site should have enough oxygen to at least:

options.a: Transport at least three divers to emergency medical care
options.b: Transport a diver to emergency medical care
options.c: Support a victim for a three-hour boat ride
options.d: Support at least two victims for a one-hour boat ride

explanation: Minimum oxygen supply must be sufficient to transport a diver to emergency medical care.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Which of the following injuries is not caused by breathing compressed gas?

options.a: Arterial Gas Embolism
options.b: Pneumothorax
options.c: Sea Sickness
options.d: Decompression Sickness

explanation: Sea sickness is motion-induced, not caused by breathing compressed gas; the others are dive-related injuries.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Which injury is caused by gas trapped between the lung and the chest cavity?

options.a: Decompression Sickness
options.b: Pneumothorax
options.c: Arterial Gas Embolism
options.d: Subcutaneous Emphysema

explanation: Pneumothorax is gas trapped between the lung and the chest cavity, collapsing the lung.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: What is the primary consideration for treating a lung overexpansion injury?

options.a: Get the victim under shade
options.b: Recompress the victim at five meters
options.c: Provide supplemental oxygen
options.d: Immediately fly the victim to emergency medical services

explanation: Provide supplemental (high-concentration) oxygen as the primary first aid for lung overexpansion injuries.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: A victim of decompression sickness may:

options.a: Require recompression in a hyperbaric chamber
options.b: Never dive again
options.c: Immediately begin diving again
options.d: Require in-water recompression

explanation: Decompression sickness is treated by recompression in a hyperbaric chamber under medical supervision.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Which of the following is a general guideline for managing stress before the dive?

options.a: Only take divers on dives they are qualified for
options.b: Help everyone make a safe and thorough dive plan
options.c: Create a fun, positive atmosphere
options.d: All answers are correct

explanation: All three guidelines together help manage diver stress before the dive.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Eliminating stress before starting the dive:

options.a: Keeps it from becoming worse as the dive continues
options.b: Ensures the divers will have no problems once the dive begins
options.c: Means you do not have to watch the divers as close during the dive
options.d: All answers are correct

explanation: Resolving stress before the dive prevents it from compounding once the dive begins.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Stressed divers at depth may:

options.a: Have trouble understanding hand signals
options.b: All answers are correct
options.c: Have wide, staring eyes
options.d: Breathe rapidly and shallowly

explanation: Stressed divers exhibit all of these signs: comprehension problems, wide eyes, and rapid shallow breathing.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: A diver who is panicking:

options.a: Will describe their problem to their Divemaster
options.b: Has no control over their actions
options.c: Should only be assisted if they request help
options.d: Can usually correct the problem themselves

explanation: A panicking diver has lost rational control over their actions and cannot be expected to self-rescue.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: If you observe a diver panicking:

options.a: Allow the diver to attempt to solve the problem first
options.b: Have their buddy provide assistance first
options.c: Take immediate action to correct the issue
options.d: Immediately remove the diver from the water

explanation: A panicking diver cannot solve the problem themselves; the Divemaster must take immediate action.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Your priority when assisting a panicked diver at the surface is to:

options.a: Keep their equipment in place
options.b: Establish positive buoyancy
options.c: Calm the diver down
options.d: Allow the diver to attempt to solve the problem first

explanation: Establishing positive buoyancy is the first priority for a panicked diver at the surface.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Emergencies usually occur:

options.a: In isolated situations without warning
options.b: As the culmination of several smaller problems
options.c: To the entire dive group at once
options.d: Only to very new or inexperienced divers

explanation: Emergencies are typically the result of several smaller, unmanaged problems compounding over time.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: The last-seen point is:

options.a: Always determined by the missing diver's buddy
options.b: The point where the underwater search should begin
options.c: Only important if multiple divers are missing
options.d: Only used for searches using rescue mode

explanation: The last-seen point is the location where the underwater search begins.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Choose a search pattern based on:

options.a: Your plan of action
options.b: The size of the search area
options.c: All answers are correct
options.d: Environmental and dive site conditions

explanation: Search pattern selection depends on the plan, the area size, and environmental/site conditions together.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: While you wait for emergency services to arrive, you should:

options.a: Leave the victim to signal the emergency services personnel
options.b: Start disassembling everyone's equipment
options.c: Stay with the victim and provide care
options.d: Report the incident to your training center

explanation: Stay with the victim and continue providing care until emergency medical services arrive.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: Local authorities should:

options.a: Wait until emergency medical services arrive to take control
options.b: Allow the Divemaster to manage the scene
options.c: Not be involved with incident management
options.d: Take control of the scene once they arrive

explanation: Local authorities take control of the scene as soon as they arrive.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: What should you record on the incident report form?

options.a: Every fact that you learn at the scene
options.b: Facts and opinions that you learn at the scene
options.c: Only information about the victim
options.d: Only information about the scene management

explanation: Record only facts (no opinions) gathered at the scene on the incident report form.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: If you take possession of the victim's equipment:

options.a: Return it to the victim or their family as soon as possible
options.b: Record its condition and secure it in a safe location
options.c: Provide it to the emergency medical services personnel
options.d: Give a receipt to the local authorities if they request one

explanation: Document the condition of the victim's equipment and secure it in a safe location for investigation.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: The purpose of the SSI Snorkel Diver program is to:

options.a: Develop new candidates for the SSI Freediver program
options.b: Provide an alternative to the SSI Scuba Diver program
options.c: Provide a basic introduction to snorkeling equipment and skills
options.d: All answers are correct

explanation: The SSI Snorkel Diver program offers a basic introduction to snorkeling equipment and skills.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: You will be issued the _____ certification after completing this program.

options.a: Professional Divemaster
options.b: Dive Guide
options.c: Certified assistant
options.d: Snorkel Instructor

explanation: Completing this program issues the Dive Guide certification (entry to the professional path).
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A

question: What is required to upgrade to Divemaster?

options.a: Study the Science of Diving materials and pass the digital final exam
options.b: Complete 100 dives and pass the Ecology exam
options.c: Teach beginners and lead key programs
options.d: Log at least 50 dives and complete a specialty instructor course

explanation: To upgrade Dive Guide to Divemaster, study the Science of Diving materials and pass the digital final exam.
source: Personal: SSI Dive Guide Exam Form A
